commands.review_summary:
The PR changes device pairing approval so valid same-key untagged device tokens can be reused during metadata-refresh approvals while unsafe token states still rotate, with regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +77, Tests +189. Total +266 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. current main source shows approveDevicePairing always mints a replacement token for requested roles when an existing token is present, and the PR adds a focused metadata-refresh test for stable token reuse. I did not run the test because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer draft changes auth-token rotation semantics, so the next action is maintainer validation and final proof rather than an automated repair PR.

target.title: fix(pairing): preserve stable device tokens on metadata refresh
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve existing untagged device tokens for same-key metadata refresh approvals when the token was already valid before the approval
- keep rotating for scope expansion, public-key repair, stale baseline tokens, revoked/issuer-tagged tokens, incompatible role/scopes, and malformed persisted token entries
- add pairing token regression coverage for Windows node reconnect churn and unsafe reuse boundaries

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/device-pairing.test.ts`: 60 passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- maintainer Crabbox `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`: passed core/coreTests lanes

## Notes
Draft because the signed prerelease recut plus full pond pairing / exec-approval validation are still pending.
target.author: vincentkoc
